title: Date calculated by adding a value determined by a label to a date

posts.author: jwc1980 (John Campbell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I have the following columns; ‘Date Added’, ‘Priority’ and ‘Date Forecast’.

Each priority has a time associated with it: A=1, B=2, C=3, D=4

Depending on which label is selected from the ‘Priority’ column, I would like for the associated days to be added to the ‘Date Added’ value and to then be displayed as a result in a ‘Date Forecast’ column.

First day using Monday.com so really appreciate any help offered.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jwc1980 - Is Date Added the actual date the item as added and the Date Forecast is the Date Added + corresponding Priority value? Depending on the answer you may need a different solution. If the Date Forecast already has a value or is based on current date its easy.

When Priority Changes to A, set Date Forecast to today and push Date Forecast by 1 day
When Priority Changes to B, set Date Forecast to today and push Date Forecast by 2 days
When Priority Changes to C, set Date Forecast to today and push Date Forecast by 3 days
When Priority Changes to D, set Date Forecast to today and push Date Forecast by 4 days

If not, and the date is blank and based on the Date Added, you may need so assistance from an app.

Let me know.
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

posts.author: jwc1980 (John Campbell)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Mark, thank you for the support. I discovered automations this morning, which is so much easier than formula based. In the words of my 9 year old; I am such a noob!

posts.author: FredBaker (Integrated Human | Authorized Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: lol! Welcome to monday.com! It is a super capable platform, and always growing! If you run into something it can’t do chances are it will be added within the next year or so.
